# Omni-Agent Phase 4C â€” Gemini OAuth Provider Integration
<!-- Generated by conv2spec | Source: conversation | Date: 2026-04-23 -->

## Project Context
Omni-Agent (Cindy) currently calls the Gemini API using a `GEMINI_API_KEY` (pay-per-token billing). Icekimo holds a paid Google One AI Premium / Gemini Advanced subscription whose quota is accessible via OAuth 2.0 credentials previously generated by `gemini-cli`. This phase adds an `OAuthGeminiClient` to the Brain service so that Cindy uses the Pro subscription quota by default, falling back to the API key only when OAuth refresh fails.

## Scope
**In scope:** `OAuthGeminiClient` implementation, token refresh logic, PostgreSQL access-token cache (`oauth_tokens` table), `.env` variable `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN`, routing config update to prefer OAuth provider, fallback to `GEMINI_API_KEY`

**Out of scope:** gemini-cli subprocess / subagent invocation, Telegram streaming (edit-in-place), any changes to Gateway or Skills Server, Home Assistant, WoL

**Assumptions:** `refresh_token` in `.env` is valid and not revoked; Google OAuth 2.0 token endpoint is reachable from the Brain container; existing `GeminiClient` (API key path) remains unchanged and is used as fallback; `gemini-2.5-pro` is the target model for the OAuth provider (Google may serve a newer model transparently); Podman deployment on Debian 13

---

## Functional Requirements

### Task: Add `oauth_tokens` table to PostgreSQL
**Requirement:** A new table must persist the OAuth access token and its expiry so that Brain containers survive restarts without immediately calling the refresh endpoint.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a fresh database, when the Brain migration runs, then the `oauth_tokens` table exists with columns `provider TEXT PRIMARY KEY`, `access_token TEXT`, `refresh_token TEXT`, `expiry_ms BIGINT`, `updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ`.
- [ ] Given the table exists, when Brain reads the `gemini` row, then it returns the stored access token and expiry.
- [ ] Edge case: Given the table exists but has no `gemini` row, when Brain starts up, then it proceeds to refresh immediately without crashing.

---

### Task: Implement `OAuthGeminiClient`
**Requirement:** A new LLM client class must authenticate to the Gemini API using an OAuth Bearer token instead of an API key, with automatic token refresh.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` is set in `.env`, when `OAuthGeminiClient` is instantiated, then it reads the variable and does not require `GEMINI_API_KEY` to function.
- [ ] Given a valid (non-expired) access token exists in the `oauth_tokens` DB row, when `chat()` is called, then the request is sent using that token without calling the refresh endpoint.
- [ ] Given the stored access token is expired (`expiry_ms < now_ms`), when `chat()` is called, then the client calls the Google OAuth refresh endpoint, stores the new token and updated expiry in DB, and proceeds with the request.
- [ ] Given the refresh endpoint returns a new token, when the DB write succeeds, then subsequent calls within the token's lifetime do not trigger another refresh.
- [ ] Edge case: Given the refresh endpoint returns an error (network failure, revoked token), when the exception is caught, then `OAuthGeminiClient` raises a recoverable `OAuthRefreshError` and does NOT silently return an empty response.

---

### Task: Token refresh implementation
**Requirement:** The token refresh mechanism must call Google's OAuth 2.0 token endpoint with the stored `refresh_token` and update the DB cache atomically.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a valid `refresh_token`, when the refresh call is made, then it POSTs to `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token` with `grant_type=refresh_token`, `client_id`, and `client_secret` (sourced from env or hardcoded gemini-cli public client values).
- [ ] Given a successful refresh response, when the new `access_token` and `expires_in` are returned, then `expiry_ms` is stored as `now_ms + (expires_in * 1000)`.
- [ ] Given a concurrent Brain process also attempts refresh, when both read an expired token simultaneously, then only one refresh call is made (use DB `SELECT FOR UPDATE` or upsert with timestamp guard).
- [ ] Edge case: Given `expires_in` is absent from the response, when storing expiry, then default to `now + 3500 seconds` (conservative 1-hour assumption).

---

### Task: Wire `OAuthGeminiClient` into `ModelRouter`
**Requirement:** The `ModelRouter` must prefer the OAuth Gemini provider when available and fall back to the API-key `GeminiClient` on `OAuthRefreshError`.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` is present in env, when `ModelRouter` initialises, then `OAuthGeminiClient` is registered as the primary Gemini provider.
- [ ] Given `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` is absent from env, when `ModelRouter` initialises, then the existing `GeminiClient` (API key) is used as primary with no error.
- [ ] Given `OAuthGeminiClient.chat()` raises `OAuthRefreshError`, when the router handles the exception, then it falls back to `GeminiClient` (API key) and logs a `WARNING` with the reason.
- [ ] Given fallback to `GeminiClient` occurs, when the response is returned, then `routing_reason` in `BrainResponse` reflects `"gemini_oauth_fallback"`.
- [ ] Edge case: Given both OAuth and API key are unavailable, when all Gemini providers fail, then the existing Claude â†’ local fallback chain is invoked unchanged.

---

### Task: Update `routing_config.json`
**Requirement:** The Brain routing configuration must declare the OAuth Gemini provider so that model selection and fallback order are explicit and git-tracked.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given the updated `routing_config.json`, when parsed, then a `gemini_oauth` provider entry exists with `auth_type: "oauth"` and `model: "gemini-2.5-pro"`.
- [ ] Given `gemini_oauth` is the primary provider, when `routing_config.json` is loaded, then the fallback order is `gemini_oauth â†’ gemini_apikey â†’ claude â†’ local`.
- [ ] Edge case: Given `routing_config.json` is malformed or missing, when Brain starts, then it falls back to hardcoded defaults (existing behaviour) without crashing.

---

### Task: `.env` documentation update
**Requirement:** The `.env.example` file must document the new `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` variable so future deployments are self-documenting.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given `.env.example` is opened, when read, then `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` appears with a comment explaining it is the OAuth refresh token from `~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`.
- [ ] Given `.env.example` is opened, when read, then a comment notes the two conditions requiring manual update: manual Google account revocation, or >6 months of inactivity.
- [ ] Given `GEMINI_API_KEY` is present in `.env.example`, when read, then it is annotated as fallback-only when `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` is set.

---

## Non-Functional Requirements

### Task: Security â€” no credential leakage
**Requirement:** The `refresh_token` and `access_token` must never appear in application logs or error messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given any log level (DEBUG through ERROR), when Brain is running, then no log line contains the literal `refresh_token` value or `access_token` value.
- [ ] Given an `OAuthRefreshError` is logged, when the log entry is written, then it contains only the error type and HTTP status code, not the token strings.
- [ ] Edge case: Given an exception traceback is logged, when it includes a requests payload, then the token fields are masked as `[REDACTED]`.

---

### Task: Startup performance â€” avoid blocking refresh on boot
**Requirement:** Brain container startup must not block waiting for an OAuth token refresh if the cached token is still valid.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given a valid non-expired token exists in DB, when Brain starts, then the first `/health` response is returned within normal startup time (no additional OAuth round-trip).
- [ ] Given no token exists in DB on first boot, when Brain starts, then it performs one refresh call during initialisation before accepting requests, and logs `INFO: OAuth token initialised`.

---

## Integration Requirements

### Task: PostgreSQL migration for `oauth_tokens`
**Requirement:** The `oauth_tokens` table must be created via the existing Brain migration mechanism, consistent with current schema management patterns.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given `podman compose up` is run on a clean DB, when Brain starts, then the `oauth_tokens` table is created automatically without manual SQL intervention.
- [ ] Given the migration runs on a DB that already has `oauth_tokens`, when Brain starts, then the migration is idempotent (no error, no data loss).

---

### Task: Google OAuth 2.0 endpoint reachability
**Requirement:** The Brain container must be able to reach `https://oauth2.googleapis.com` for token refresh.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Given the Brain container is running, when a token refresh is triggered, then the POST to `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token` succeeds with HTTP 200.
- [ ] Edge case: Given the endpoint is unreachable (network partition), when the POST times out after 10 seconds, then `OAuthRefreshError` is raised and the fallback chain activates.

---

## Testing Notes

- âš™ď¸Ź Use `podman compose` throughout â€” not `docker compose`.
- âš™ď¸Ź Deployment target is Debian 13 with Podman containers.
- âš™ď¸Ź `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` must be manually copied from `~/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` on the host (`refresh_token` field) into `.env` before running integration tests.
- âš™ď¸Ź The `oauth_creds.json` file on the host is **not** mounted into any container â€” Brain reads exclusively from `.env` and the `oauth_tokens` DB table.
- âš™ď¸Ź To test token-expired path: temporarily set `expiry_ms` to `0` in the `oauth_tokens` DB row via `psql`, then send a chat request.
- âš™ď¸Ź To test fallback path: temporarily set `GEMINI_REFRESH_TOKEN` to an invalid string in `.env` and restart Brain; verify `routing_reason: "gemini_oauth_fallback"` appears in response.
- âš™ď¸Ź Local LLM (`omlx`) is skipped in CI â€” set `OMNI_ENV=test` as usual.
- âš™ď¸Ź Do not run integration tests that call Google OAuth from CI/CD â€” these require live credentials and network access to `oauth2.googleapis.com`.

---

## Open Questions
> No blocking open questions. All design decisions resolved in conversation.

- â„ąď¸Ź `gemini-cli` subagent delegation (Cindy orchestrating gemini-cli as a subagent) is explicitly deferred to a future phase â€” do not include in this implementation.
